note: Simplify hides selected built-in form elements from entity add/edit forms. Its global state lives in the simplify.global config object: a boolean simplify_admin plus one array-of-string key per entity type (simplify_nodes_global, simplify_users_global, simplify_comments_global, simplify_taxonomies_global, simplify_blocks_global, simplify_media_global, simplify_menu_links_global, simplify_profiles_global, simplify_eck_global). The valid element keys are fixed in simplify_get_fields() (e.g. nodes: author, options, revision_information, meta, format, path, menu, comment, book; users: status, timezone, format, contact). Per-bundle overrides live in simplify.content_type.*, simplify.comment_type.*, simplify.vocabulary.*, simplify.block_type.*. Two permissions: administer simplify (settings form) and view hidden fields (bypass hiding). Introspection cases save a known simplify.global then read it back; execution cases reset simplify.global and require the agent to write the correct element keys, verified live.

evals.prompt: Using the Drupal contrib module Simplify (drupal/simplify), name at least three of the built-in node edit-form sections/elements it can hide from content editors.
evals.expected_output: On node forms Simplify can hide: Authoring information (author), Text format selection (format), Promotion options (options), Revision information (revision_information), Status metadata (meta), and — when the relevant module is on — Book outline, Comment settings, Menu settings, URL path settings (path) and Content translation.

evals.prompt: I enabled the Simplify module on Drupal 11. Which configuration object stores the site-wide (global) list of fields to hide, and what is the config key that holds the elements hidden from all node forms?
evals.expected_output: The global settings live in the config object simplify.global (edited at /admin/config/user-interface/simplify, route simplify.admin). The node elements hidden on every node form are stored as an array of keys under simplify_nodes_global.

evals.prompt: In the Simplify module, which permission lets a user bypass Simplify entirely and still see all the hidden fields, and which permission is needed to reach its configuration form?
evals.expected_output: The 'view hidden fields' permission exempts a user from Simplify (they see every hidden field). The 'administer simplify' permission grants access to the settings form and the per-bundle Simplify sections.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site, Simplify has been configured to hide some elements from all node edit forms. Inspect the live Simplify configuration and tell me which node-form elements are currently being hidden globally.
evals.expected_output: simplify.global:simplify_nodes_global is set to [author, options, revision_information] — i.e. Authoring information, Promotion options and Revision information are hidden on all node forms.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site with Simplify enabled, is the module currently configured to ALSO hide fields from administrator users (User 1 and users with an admin role), or do admins still see everything? Inspect the live Simplify configuration to answer.
evals.expected_output: Yes — simplify.global:simplify_admin is set to TRUE ('Hide fields from admin users' is on), so admin/User 1 are also subject to Simplify. (simplify_users_global also has [status], hiding the Status control on user forms.)

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site, configure the Simplify module so that the 'Authoring information' and 'Promotion options' sections are hidden from ALL node add/edit forms site-wide (the global setting, not a per-content-type override). Apply the change to the live site.
evals.expected_output: simplify.global:simplify_nodes_global contains both 'author' (Authoring information) and 'options' (Promotion options), so both sections are hidden on every node form.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site with Simplify enabled, configure it so that the 'Status (blocked/active)' control is hidden from the user account forms site-wide. Apply the change to the live site's Simplify global configuration.
evals.expected_output: simplify.global:simplify_users_global contains 'status', so the account Status (blocked/active) control is hidden on user add/edit forms.
